burial.plot_notes: Ware Episcopal Church Cemetery, Gloucester VA.

parents.father_research_notes: Gillet Lefferts Sr. — implied by Jr. suffix.

military_service.notes: Honorably served as Airplane Commander in the Army Air Force during WWII.

career.notes: Consultant role late in career. Retired

narrative_blocks.header: He rode the same train 57 years
narrative_blocks.body: An architect who shaped a New York firm for half a century, he flew as an Army Air Force airplane commander in the war and lived to ninety-seven. He kept catching the same Metro-North train into the city for fifty-seven years, until he retired at eighty-eight.

burial.plot_notes: Ware Episcopal Church Cemetery, Gloucester VA.

narrative_blocks.header: He rode the same train 57 years
narrative_blocks.body: An architect who shaped a New York firm for half a century, he flew as an Army Air Force airplane commander in the war and lived to ninety-seven. He kept catching the same Metro-North train into the city for fifty-seven years, until he retired at eighty-eight.

burial.plot_notes: Ware Episcopal Church Cemetery, Gloucester VA.

residence.notes: Moorestown NJ → Philadelphia (during medical art career) → New York City (1951 marriage) → Darien, CT (1955 onward).

education.notes: Same school attended by her aunt Lucia Beverly Hollerith II (TD0091).

career.notes: Worked under Dr. C. Everett Koop (later U.S. Surgeon General) prior to her 1951 marriage. Notable mentorship — Koop was a pioneer in pediatric surgery at CHOP.
Ran the AV Department at Darien High School in the early 1980s.
Produced numerous segments for Darien Dateline.

narrative_blocks.header: Worked for Dr Koop before her 1951 marriage
narrative_blocks.body: Before her 1951 marriage to Gil Lefferts Jr she worked as a medical artist and photographer at The Children's Hospital of Philadelphia — under Dr C Everett Koop, who would later become US Surgeon General. The career was a Connecticut College Art-major to medical-illustration trajectory.

narrative_blocks.header: A renaissance woman of post-war Darien
narrative_blocks.body: C.C. trained as a medical artist and photographer under future U.S. Surgeon General C. Everett Koop at Children's Hospital of Philadelphia before her 1951 marriage. In Darien CT she raised five children, ran the high school AV department, produced segments for public-access TV, and wrote handwritten ads for the Darien Sport Shop that adorned a wall on Leroy Avenue for decades.

narrative_blocks.header: Her handwriting adorned Darien Sport Shop for decades
narrative_blocks.body: She produced ads for local Darien businesses including the Darien Sport Shop — her hand-lettering adorned the storefront wall along Leroy Avenue for decades. The graphic work continued alongside her AV and arts careers from the 1960s into the early 1980s.

burialCemetery.name: Ware Episcopal Church Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Ware Episcopal Church Cemetery — burial site of multiple Hollerith descendants and their spouses.

INST023.name: Hopkins Academy
INST023.notes: Hadley, Massachusetts academy founded from the bequest of Edward Hopkins — the same bequest behind Hopkins Grammar School, New Haven (INST001), but a DISTINCT institution. Roger Hooker Leavitt and Hart Leavitt are graduates; compiler flagged the distinction Stage 6Y-687.

INST043.name: Williams College
INST043.notes: Private liberal arts college founded 1793 with funds from the estate of Ephraim Williams, a colonist killed in the French and Indian War (1755). One of the original "Little Three" liberal arts colleges.
